steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage links cooler months to shorter lifespan via a correlation between wing centroid size and lifespan.

The decisive clue is the claim that "flies collected in cooler months should tend to have a shorter lifespan"—this matches data showing smaller wing sizes (proxy for smaller body size) in cooler months.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice A shows the average female wing size was smaller in May than in June.

Since May is typically cooler than June, this aligns with the assertion’s logic: smaller wing sizes in cooler months → shorter lifespan.

The other three choices either focus on irrelevant comparisons (male vs. female, temperature alone, unrelated timeframes) or reverse the temperature-size relationship.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
